message:
fix(tools,install): harden reservation conflict detection and improve install.sh robustness

Concurrent agent improvements:
- reservations.rs: Improved file reservation conflict detection logic
  with better overlap checking and error messages
- install.sh: Enhanced installation robustness with better alias/function
  detection, multi-shell config handling, and fallback paths (+69 lines)
- Beads issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -604,10 +604,10 @@
 {"id":"br-28mgh.2.2","title":"T2.2: Integrate Python displacement into install flow","description":"Wire the Python detection and displacement functions (Track 1) into the main
install.sh flow. The sequence should be:

1. preflight_checks() (existing)
2. detect_python_installation() (NEW - from T1)
3. If Python found:
   a. Print clear message: "Existing Python mcp-agent-mail detected"
   b. In non-interactive mode: auto-migrate
   c. In interactive mode: prompt "Migrate from Python to Rust? [Y/n]"
   d. If yes: run displacement (T1.4), stop server (T1.5), queue migration (T3)
   e. If no: warn that am alias will shadow the Rust binary
4. Install Rust binaries (existing)
5. Run database migration if needed (T3)
6. Update MCP configs if needed (T4)
7. Post-install verification (T2.4)

File: install.sh (modify main flow around line 510)
Depends on: T1.1, T1.2, T1.3, T1.4, T1.5","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:49.643462669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:26:03.029305606Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:26:03.029237628Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:13.817638448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:14.126105748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:14.429803229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:14.738731733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:15.046397931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:49.643462669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:09:38.664457304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:09:39.378687251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":908,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Add rollback-on-failure logic. If any step in the migration sequence fails (displacement, DB migration, MCP config update), the installer should: (1) Report which step failed, (2) Restore rc file from backup if alias displacement was done, (3) NOT leave the system in a half-migrated state. The sequence should be: detect -> backup everything -> displace/migrate/update -> verify. If verify fails, offer rollback. This is critical for user trust.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:10:44Z"},{"id":925,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Critical sequencing concern: MCP agent tools (Claude Code, etc.) may have active sessions using the Python server. The migration should: (1) Update MCP configs AFTER binaries are installed but BEFORE printing success, (2) Warn user that active MCP sessions will need to be restarted, (3) Consider: should we stop the Rust server if it auto-started and the migration failed? The goal is to never leave the user with a non-functional am command.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:13:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.2.3","title":"T2.3: Atomic binary replacement","description":"Current install uses `install -m 0755 $SRC $DEST/am` which overwrites in-place.
If the system crashes mid-write, the binary is corrupted. Fix:

1. Write to temporary file: install -m 0755 $SRC $DEST/.am.tmp.$$
2. Sync to disk: sync (or fsync if available)
3. Atomic rename: mv $DEST/.am.tmp.$$ $DEST/am
4. Same for mcp-agent-mail binary
5. Clean up any stale .tmp files from previous failed installs

On Windows (if we add PowerShell installer), use Move-Item -Force which is atomic on NTFS.

File: install.sh (modify lines 657-658)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:50.075652262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:24:06.446967902Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:24:06.446888624Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.3","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:50.075652262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.2.4","title":"T2.4: Post-install verification","description":"After installation, verify that everything actually works:

1. Check that $DEST/am exists and is executable
2. Run `$DEST/am --version` and verify it returns a Rust version string
3. Check that `type am` (in a fresh shell context) resolves to $DEST/am, NOT an alias
   - This requires spawning a subshell that sources the rc file:
     bash -l -c "type am" or zsh -l -c "type am"
4. If alias still wins, print LOUD warning with fix instructions
5. Check that mcp-agent-mail is also accessible
6. If --verify flag: run `am doctor` for full health check

This is the critical "did it actually work?" step. Without it, the user gets a green
"installed\!" message but the old Python version still runs.

File: install.sh (new function verify_installation(), called after binary install)
Depends on: T2.3 (binaries must be installed first)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:50.399657861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:27:14.055379112Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:27:14.055308800Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:50.399657861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:15.361833729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":907,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Post-install verification should also check MCP configs were updated (if migration was performed). Add step: If Python was detected and displaced, verify at least one MCP config now points to the Rust binary. Also verify the rc file backup was created and Python alias is no longer active.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:09:47Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-28mgh.2.5","title":"T2.5: Uninstall flag","description":"Add --uninstall flag to install.sh that cleanly removes everything:

1. Remove binaries: $DEST/am, $DEST/mcp-agent-mail
2. Remove PATH entry from shell rc files (if added by installer)
3. Optionally remove database and storage (prompt user or --purge flag)
4. Optionally restore Python alias (if backup exists)
5. Remove MCP config entries for Rust binary
6. Print summary of what was removed

Interactive mode: confirm each destructive step
Non-interactive (--yes): remove everything except data (require --purge for data)

File: install.sh (new function uninstall(), called early if --uninstall is passed)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:50.701929052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:27.704556069Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.5","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:50.701929052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":917,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Uninstall should also: (1) Remove mcp-agent-mail entry from all MCP configs (or offer to), (2) Remove the update-check cache file, (3) Remove any log files in /tmp/am-install-*, (4) Offer to restore the Python alias if a backup exists. The --purge flag should list exactly what data will be deleted and its size before confirming.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:27Z"}]}

The --purge flag should list exactly what data will be deleted and its size before confirming.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.2.6","title":"T2.6: Windows PowerShell installer","description":"Create install.ps1 for Windows users. The bash installer cannot run on Windows
natively. PowerShell is the standard Windows scripting environment.

Scope:
1. Download Windows zip from GitHub releases
2. Extract to %LOCALAPPDATA%/Programs/mcp-agent-mail/ or user-specified dir
3. Add to User PATH (via [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable)
4. Verify installation
5. Handle sqlite3.dll bundling (must be next to .exe)
6. Support -Version, -Dest, -Force parameters
7. Support iwr (Invoke-WebRequest) pipe pattern:
   iwr -useb https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.ps1 | iex

File: install.ps1 (NEW file at repo root)
Note: Lower priority since Windows is not the primary target platform.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:51.009066114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:31.565518416Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.6","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:51.009066114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":918,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: The Windows PowerShell installer also needs to handle Python displacement, but the mechanism is different: Python on Windows uses pip-installed console_scripts (not shell aliases). Check for: (1) python -m mcp_agent_mail in PATH, (2) am.exe in Python Scripts/ directory (pip install creates this), (3) Python virtualenv at common locations. Displacement means removing/renaming the Python am.exe in Scripts/ and ensuring the Rust am.exe is found first in PATH.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.2.7","title":"T2.7: Improve error messages with remediation","description":"Every error path in install.sh should include actionable remediation steps.
Audit all err() calls and ensure each one tells the user WHAT TO DO:

Current bad pattern:
  err "Binary not found in archive"

Better:
  err "Binary not found in archive"
  err ""
  err "This usually means the release tarball is malformed."
  err "Try:"
  err "  1. Re-run the installer: curl -fsSL ... | bash"
  err "  2. Use --force to skip version check"
  err "  3. Report at https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail_rust/issues"

Also add a final catch-all trap that prints a help URL on any unexpected failure:
  trap 'err "Unexpected error. Report: https://github.com/..."; exit 1' ERR

File: install.sh (audit all err() calls, ~15 locations)","notes":"2026-02-27 BlackElm triage: evaluated global ERR-trap approach for catch-all remediation messaging but rejected it due false-positive exits in grep-probe flows under set -euo pipefail. No trap-based change retained. Next step is targeted per-error remediation text updates without introducing control-flow side effects.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackElm","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:51.311939573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:27:02.072285473Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:17:51.311939573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-28mgh.2.8","title":"T2.8: Installer verbose logging mode","description":"Add --verbose flag to install.sh that enables detailed step-by-step logging. Currently the installer only prints high-level messages. When debugging installation issues, we need granular logging of every decision point. With --verbose: print each detection function inputs/outputs, PATH resolution details, file modification diffs, download progress, checksum verification, migration step row counts and timing. All log lines prefixed with [VERBOSE]. Without --verbose: current behavior, but on any error auto-dump the last 20 verbose log lines. Implementation: Write all verbose output to a temp log file (/tmp/am-install-TIMESTAMP.log). On error, print the log file path and last N lines. File: install.sh (add LOG_FILE variable, log() function, --verbose flag parsing)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayDeer","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:10:19.509462195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:30:58.512274039Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.8","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:10:19.509462195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

File: install.sh (add LOG_FILE variable, log() function, --verbose flag parsing)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayDeer","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:10:19.509462195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:36:16.535418352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:36:16.535398756Z","close_reason":"Implemented --verbose installer diagnostics in install.sh: persistent /tmp verbose log, [VERBOSE] prefixed traces, download/checksum/sigstore/path/detection/migration diagnostics, and automatic last-20-line verbose tail dump on error. Validated with bash -n and help/failure-path smoke checks.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2.8","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:10:19.509462195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.3","title":"T3: Database Migration","description":"Track 3: Database Migration (Python TEXT timestamps to Rust i64 microseconds)

The Python version stores timestamps as TEXT (ISO-8601 naive UTC datetimes like
"2026-02-24 15:30:00.123456"). The Rust version uses i64 (microseconds since Unix epoch).
A seamless migration must convert all timestamps without data loss.

## Scope
- Implement `am migrate` CLI command
- Auto-detect Python database format (TEXT timestamps)
- Convert all timestamp columns across all tables
- Rebuild FTS5 virtual tables (cannot be copied directly)
- Backup original database before migration
- Verify data integrity post-migration (row counts, spot checks)
- Handle edge cases: NULL timestamps, pre-1970 dates, malformed strings
- Idempotent: running migrate twice is safe (detects already-migrated)
- Report migration summary (tables, rows converted, time taken)

## Tables with Timestamps
- projects: created_at
- agents: created_at
- messages: created_at, updated_at
- message_recipients: read_at, ack_ts
- file_reservations: created_at, expires_at, released_at
- agent_links: created_at, updated_at, expires_at
- message_summaries: created_at
- product_project_links: created_at

## Conversion
Python: "2026-02-24 15:30:00.123456" (TEXT, naive UTC)
Rust: 1772106600123456 (i64, microseconds since epoch)
Use chrono NaiveDateTime parsing with div_euclid/rem_euclid for safety.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleCliff","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:31.334829158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:17:53.206335852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:31.334829158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.3.1","title":"T3.1: Detect database timestamp format","description":"Implement a function that opens a SQLite database and determines whether
timestamps are stored as TEXT (Python format) or INTEGER (Rust format).

Detection logic:
1. Open the database read-only
2. Query: SELECT typeof(created_at) FROM messages LIMIT 1
3. If result is "text" -> Python format, needs migration
4. If result is "integer" -> Rust format, already migrated
5. If table is empty, check agents or projects table
6. If all tables are empty, return "empty" (no migration needed)
7. Also check: does the TEXT look like ISO-8601? ("2026-02-24 15:30:00.123456")

Return: MigrationNeeded | AlreadyMigrated | EmptyDatabase | UnknownFormat

File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/migrate.rs (NEW module)
Also: install.sh should call `am migrate --check` to detect format

Why this is separate from T3.2: Detection must work without modification,
so it can be called by install.sh and by `am doctor` independently.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:24.437180882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:57:06.632454092Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T06:57:06.632389781Z","close_reason":"Implemented detect_timestamp_format() and detect_column_format() in mcp-agent-mail-db/src/migrate.rs. Probes all 8 timestamp tables via typeof(). Returns RustMicros|PythonText|Empty|Mixed|Unknown. 5 detection tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:24.437180882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":930,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by RubyPrairie. Starting implementation: add migrate.rs module to mcp-agent-mail-db with timestamp format detection (typeof() queries across all tables). Will also wire into CLI as 'am migrate --check'.","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:44:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.3.2","title":"T3.2: Implement timestamp conversion functions","description":"Implement the core timestamp conversion logic for Python TEXT to Rust i64.

Python format examples:
- "2026-02-24 15:30:00.123456" (most common: space separator, microseconds)
- "2026-02-24T15:30:00.123456" (ISO-8601 with T separator)
- "2026-02-24 15:30:00" (no fractional seconds)
- "2026-02-24" (date only, seen in some expires_at fields)
- NULL (preserve as NULL)

Conversion: Parse with chrono::NaiveDateTime, convert to i64 microseconds.
Use div_euclid/rem_euclid for pre-1970 safety (already implemented in timestamps.rs).

Functions needed:
- text_to_micros(text: &str) -> Result<i64, MigrationError>
- convert_column(conn, table, column) -> Result<u64, MigrationError> (returns rows converted)

Edge cases to handle:
- Empty string -> treat as NULL
- Malformed timestamp -> log warning, skip row, continue
- Timestamp with timezone suffix -> strip and treat as UTC
- Pre-1970 timestamps -> supported via div_euclid

File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/migrate.rs
Depends on: T3.1 (reuses detection)

Tests: At least 10 conversion vectors covering all format variations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:24.728487743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:57:15.194633085Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T06:57:15.194613789Z","close_reason":"Implemented text_to_micros(), convert_column(), convert_all_timestamps() in mcp-agent-mail-db/src/migrate.rs. Handles space/T separator, microseconds/milliseconds, date-only, timezone stripping, pre-epoch, empty strings. 21 tests total for conversion (14 parse + 4 column + 2 full migration + 1 idempotent). 0 clippy warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:24.728487743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:15.671864147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -51,6 +51,7 @@ OFFLINE="${AM_OFFLINE:-0}"
 VERBOSE_DUMP_LINES=20
 LOG_FILE="${LOG_FILE:-/tmp/am-install-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)-$$.log}"
 LOG_INITIALIZED=0
+ERROR_TAIL_EMITTED=0
 ORIGINAL_ARGS=("$@")
 
 # T2.1: Auto-enable easy-mode for pipe installs (stdin is not a terminal)
@@ -137,6 +138,8 @@ verbose() {
 }
 
 dump_verbose_tail() {
+  [ "$ERROR_TAIL_EMITTED" -eq 1 ] && return 0
+  ERROR_TAIL_EMITTED=1
   [ "$LOG_INITIALIZED" -eq 1 ] || return 0
   [ -f "$LOG_FILE" ] || return 0
   err "Verbose log: $LOG_FILE"
@@ -157,6 +160,13 @@ on_error() {
   exit "$exit_code"
 }
 
+early_exit_dump() {
+  local rc=$?
+  if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
+    dump_verbose_tail
+  fi
+}
+
 download_to_file() {
   local url="$1"
   local out="$2"
@@ -1296,16 +1306,51 @@ Options:
 EOFU
 }
 
+trap 'on_error $LINENO' ERR
+trap early_exit_dump EXIT
+init_verbose_log
+verbose "argv=${ORIGINAL_ARGS[*]:-(none)}"
+
 while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
   case "$1" in
-    --version) VERSION="$2"; shift 2;;
-    --dest) DEST="$2"; shift 2;;
+    --version)
+      if [ $# -lt 2 ]; then
+        err "Option --version requires a value"
+        dump_verbose_tail
+        exit 2
+      fi
+      VERSION="$2"; shift 2;;
+    --dest)
+      if [ $# -lt 2 ]; then
+        err "Option --dest requires a value"
+        dump_verbose_tail
+        exit 2
+      fi
+      DEST="$2"; shift 2;;
     --system) SYSTEM=1; DEST="/usr/local/bin"; shift;;
     --easy-mode) EASY=1; shift;;
     --verify) VERIFY=1; shift;;
-    --artifact-url) ARTIFACT_URL="$2"; shift 2;;
-    --checksum) CHECKSUM="$2"; shift 2;;
-    --checksum-url) CHECKSUM_URL="$2"; shift 2;;
+    --artifact-url)
+      if [ $# -lt 2 ]; then
